cards.front: ULA range split: which half is reserved, which is locally assigned?
cards.back: `FC00::/8` reserved (not yet defined). `FD00::/8` locally assigned — what you actually configure.

One-to-nearest — packet delivered to the topologically closest member.

cards.front: One-to-nearest — packet delivered to the topologically closest member.
How is an anycast address configured syntactically?
cards.back: Same as a unicast address. No special prefix or command — multiple nodes share the same address; routing reaches the nearest.
All DHCPv6 relay agents on the link.

cards.front: All DHCPv6 relay agents on the link.

cards.back: `FF02::1:FF00:0/104` — last 24 bits = last 24 bits of unicast address.

cards.front: `FF02::1:FF00:0/104` — last 24 bits = last 24 bits of unicast address.
What is the solicited-node multicast used for?
cards.back: NDP Neighbor Solicitation messages — the IPv6 replacement for ARP. Each unicast host joins its corresponding solicited-node group automatically.

cards.front: How is the solicited-node multicast for `2001:db8::AB:CDEF:1234:5678` built?
cards.back: Take last 24 bits of the unicast (`34:5678`) and append to `FF02::1:FF` → `FF02::1:FF34:5678`.
Split the 48-bit MAC address into two 24-bit halves.

cards.front: Split the 48-bit MAC address into two 24-bit halves.

cards.back: Insert `FFFE` (16 bits) between the two halves → 64-bit interface ID.

cards.front: Insert `FFFE` (16 bits) between the two halves → 64-bit interface ID.

cards.back: Flip the 7th bit (U/L bit) of the first octet.

cards.front: Flip the 7th bit (U/L bit) of the first octet.
In a MAC address, the U/L bit = 0 means

cards.back: In a MAC address, the U/L bit = 0 means

cards.front: In a MAC address, the U/L bit = 1 means

cards.back: In a MAC address, the U/L bit = 1 means

cards.front: Why flip the U/L bit when forming an IPv6 EUI-64 interface ID?
cards.back: IPv6 inverts the convention so that `1` in the IID means "this IID is universally unique." Makes universal-scope addresses easier to spot in IPv6 form.

cards.front: Worked: MAC `00:50:79:66:68:00` → IPv6 EUI-64 interface ID

cards.back: Worked: MAC `00:50:79:66:68:00` → IPv6 EUI-64 interface ID
